fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong margins and significant analyst upside, though valuation is stretched.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of 2.37 vs 2.27; next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY and analyst estimates of 236.6% for the current quarter and 300.7% for the fiscal year indicate explosive growth.
Operating margin of 21.8% and profit margin of 17.7% demonstrate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 22.8% is robust.
Mean analyst target of $1104.68 suggests significant upside from the current price.
PEG ratio of 0.63 indicates the stock may be undervalued relative to its growth.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 146.90 and Forward P/E of 45.77 are very high, indicating a premium valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 suggests significant leverage.
Free cash flow of $93.35M (TTM) is low relative to market cap and debt.
Beta of 1.53 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Lumentum (LITE) has seen mixed signals recently, with a notable analyst upgrade countered by a broader cooling in optics stocks and a report of bearish activity from a significant investor. Despite a recent dip, the company's year-to-date returns have significantly outpaced Nvidia, driven by strong AI networking demand. The stock's performance highlights its role in the AI infrastructure boom, though recent price action suggests some profit-taking or sector rotation.
